posts.author_name: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
posts.text: Managing trade-offs between food production and conservation is even more difficult than usual at high altitudes, according to a study of the Tibetan Plateau—especially when extreme weather events are considered. In PNAS: https://ow.ly/z0N350Zo9ZN

Nature research paper: Highly fragmented European wetlands with uneven restoration needs

go.nature.com/4538due
posts.external_title: Highly fragmented European wetlands with uneven restoration needs - Nature
posts.external_description: Satellite imagery and machine learning used for the mapping of six seminatural open wetland types and land-use disturbance in European countries shows that wetlands are highly fragmented and have uneven restoration needs.

posts.text: Dust concentrations increased consistently across much of Europe between 2012 and 2021, with some of the largest rises in southern Europe and the Mediterranean region, according to research in Nature. go.nature.com/44wcZjO 🌎🧪

Deep in the lush forests of the Democratic Republic of the Congo lives a rare monkey that has long evaded scientists and even most local communities. Only few have witnessed the black, orange-mouthed primate quietly perching high in the canopy. Story by @yejnlheee.bsky.social for @science.org
posts.external_title: Mysterious orange-lipped monkey discovered deep in the Congo rainforest is a new species
posts.external_description: “Likweli” find highlights importance of conservation work, researchers say

posts.author_name: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
posts.text:
🌍 #SDG 13: Climate Action

A PNAS study finds that a new climate regime is restructuring the ecology of Canada's Northern Great Lakes, highlighting how peer-reviewed research can support progress toward the UN Sustainable Development Goals.

Read the article: https://ow.ly/fFf250ZmIi4
